summary: Indicative mapping between EU AI Act obligations referenced in docs/regulatory/ai-act-obligations.json and GovAI repository artefacts. The mapping is operator-facing orientation only; legal sufficiency is determined outside GovAI.

controls.control_summary: Document a risk management lifecycle that is repeatable across releases, supported by GovAI evidence exports for individual runs.
Capture dataset provenance and lineage in dataset governance snapshots; cite GovAI evidence pack exports where they reinforce traceability.
Assemble the Annex IV dossier using the technical documentation workflow; attach reproducible exports where counsel approves.
Use the append-only audit ledger and evidence pack exports to demonstrate that lifecycle events were recorded.
Document oversight workflows: who can pause, override, or escalate, and what audit events are emitted. Implementation remains operator-owned.
Maintain accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity records and link them in the Annex IV dossier; GovAI does not certify the underlying engineering work.
Follow the conformity assessment workflow to organize provider activities prior to declaration; notified body interaction remains outside GovAI.
Establish a post-market monitoring plan referencing recurring compliance summaries and incident triggers.
Use the Annex IV section checklist in the technical documentation workflow when assembling provider dossiers.
Cross-link deployer monitoring obligations with the runtime safety and observability material; deployer compliance remains the deployer's responsibility.

mapping_caveats: Mappings are indicative; obligations under the EU AI Act and implementing acts apply directly to providers and deployers.
Control identifiers reference repository artefacts, not regulatory certification.
Some obligations require provider records that are not produced by GovAI (for example signed CE declarations).
